_global.text:
## Summary
Build a full-stack "FaithfulG Recipe Box": NestJS + Prisma/PostgreSQL API with JWT full-auth, an Angular SPA served by the same Node process, packaged into one container and deployed under the existing Colossus `/faithful-e2e-g/` ingress.

## Scope
### Files to modify
- `Dockerfile` — replace nginx-static image with a multi-stage build (build Angular → build NestJS → runtime Node image serving both).
- `nginx.conf` — delete (no longer used; NestJS serves static + SPA fallback).
- `k8s/deployment.yaml` — add container `env`/resources; keep `Recreate`; add `readinessProbe` → `/api/health`, `livenessProbe` → `/api/health`. Container listens on 80.
- `kustomization.yaml` — add `k8s/postgres.yaml` to `resources`.
- `index.html` / `README.md` — index.html moves into Angular app; README documents run/build.

### Files to create
**Backend (`backend/`)**
- `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `tsconfig.build.json`, `nest-cli.json`, `.env.example`
- `prisma/schema.prisma` — `User` and `Recipe` models.
- `prisma/seed.ts` — seed demo `User` + `Admin`; print `SEED_CREDS_JSON={...}`.
- `src/main.ts` — bootstrap, global prefix `api`, `ValidationPipe`, listen on `process.env.PORT || 80`.
- `src/app.module.ts` — wire modules + `ServeStaticModule` (serve Angular `dist`, SPA fallback, exclude `/api*`).
- `src/prisma/prisma.module.ts`, `src/prisma/prisma.service.ts`
- `src/auth/` — `auth.module.ts`, `auth.controller.ts`, `auth.service.ts`, `jwt.strategy.ts`, `jwt-auth.guard.ts`, `roles.guard.ts`, `roles.decorator.ts`, `current-user.decorator.ts`, `dto/register.dto.ts`, `dto/login.dto.ts`
- `src/users/users.service.ts`, `src/users/users.module.ts`
- `src/recipes/` — `recipes.module.ts`, `recipes.controller.ts`, `recipes.service.ts`, `dto/create-recipe.dto.ts`, `dto/update-recipe.dto.ts`
- `src/admin/admin.module.ts`, `src/admin/admin.controller.ts`, `src/admin/admin.service.ts`
- `src/health/health.controller.ts` — `/api/health`, `/api/health/deep` (deep pings DB).

**Frontend (`frontend/`)** — Angular standalone-components app
- `package.json`, `angular.json`, `tsconfig*.json`, `src/index.html` (title `FaithfulG Recipe Box`), `src/main.ts`, `src/styles.css`
- `src/app/app.config.ts` (router, `provideHttpClient(withInterceptors([authInterceptor]))`)
- `src/app/app.component.ts/html` — header branding "FaithfulG", logout.
- `src/app/app.routes.ts` — routes with `data.flow` nodes (see below).
- `src/app/core/auth.service.ts`, `core/auth.interceptor.ts`, `core/auth.guard.ts`, `core/admin.guard.ts`, `core/recipe.service.ts`, `core/admin.service.ts`, `core/models.ts`
- `src/app/pages/login/`, `pages/signup/`, `pages/recipes-list/`, `pages/recipe-form/`, `pages/admin-overview/`
- `src/environments/environment*.ts` — `apiBase: 'api'` (relative).

**Infra**
- `k8s/postgres.yaml` — Postgres 16 Deployment + ClusterIP Service (`postgres:5432`) + small PVC; modest CPU/mem requests for the namespace quota.
- `docker-entrypoint.sh` — `prisma migrate deploy` → `prisma db seed` (idempotent) → `node dist/main.js`.

### Dependencies
- Backend: `@nestjs/{common,core,platform-express,config,jwt,passport,serve-static}`, `passport`, `passport-jwt`, `@prisma/client`, `bcrypt`, `class-validator`, `class-transformer`; dev: `prisma`, `typescript`, `ts-node`, `@types/*`, `@nestjs/cli`.
- Frontend: `@angular/{core,common,router,forms,platform-browser}` ~18, `rxjs`, `zone.js`; dev: `@angular/cli`, `@angular-devkit/build-angular`, `typescript`.

### Integrations
- None (no third-party APIs/SDKs; PostgreSQL is first-party infra).

### Assumptions
- Single-container packaging: NestJS serves the API and the compiled Angular bundle; nginx removed. Container listens on 80 to match the Service.
- Angular built with `--base-href /faithful-e2e-g/` because the ingress strips that prefix; API called at relative `api/...`.
- Auth model is `full_auth`. Admin exists **only via seed** (spec mandates a seeded Admin); `POST /api/auth/register` always creates role `USER`. Recorded rather than making first-signup admin.
- Public/exempt routes: `/login`, `/signup`, `/api/health`, `/api/health/deep`, and static assets. All other API routes require JWT.
- JWT (Bearer, `Authorization` header), stored client-side in `localStorage`; stateless — fits Angular/NestJS.
- Recipe ownership: users only see/modify their own recipes (`userId` scoping); cross-user access returns 404.
- `DATABASE_URL`, `JWT_SECRET` supplied via `app-secrets`; sensible dev defaults in `.env.example`.

## Implementation Plan

### Step 1: Prisma schema & datasource
**File:** `backend/prisma/schema.prisma`
**Change:** `datasource db` (postgres, `env("DATABASE_URL")`), `generator client`. `enum Role { USER ADMIN }`. `model User { id String @id @default(cuid()); email String @unique; passwordHash String; role Role @default(USER); createdAt DateTime @default(now()); recipes Recipe[] }`. `model Recipe { id String @id @default(cuid()); title String; ingredients String; steps String; userId String; user User @relation(...); createdAt/updatedAt }`.

### Step 2: Prisma service & seed
**Files:** `backend/src/prisma/prisma.service.ts`, `backend/prisma/seed.ts`
**Change:** `PrismaService extends PrismaClient` with `onModuleInit` connect. Seed upserts a demo `USER` and `ADMIN` (bcrypt-hashed passwords), then `console.log('SEED_CREDS_JSON=' + JSON.stringify([{role, email, password}, ...]))`. Idempotent via `upsert`.

### Step 3: Auth module
**Files:** `backend/src/auth/*`
**Change:** `AuthService.register(dto)` — reject duplicate email (409), hash with bcrypt, create `USER`, return `{accessToken, user}`. `login(dto)` — verify, sign JWT `{sub, email, role}` via `JwtModule` (secret from config, `7d`). `JwtStrategy` validates + loads user. `JwtAuthGuard` (default guard), `RolesGuard` + `@Roles('ADMIN')` + `@CurrentUser()`. Controller: `POST /api/auth/register`, `POST /api/auth/login`, `GET /api/auth/me`.

### Step 4: Recipes module
**Files:** `backend/src/recipes/*`
**Change:** All routes `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard)`. `GET /api/recipes` → current user's recipes (newest first). `POST /api/recipes` (validated `{title, ingredients, steps}`). `GET/PATCH/DELETE /api/recipes/:id` scoped by `userId` (404 if not owned). Unauthenticated → **401** (satisfies scenario).

### Step 5: Admin module
**Files:** `backend/src/admin/*`
**Change:** `GET /api/admin/overview` guarded by `JwtAuthGuard` + `RolesGuard`+`@Roles('ADMIN')`. Returns `[{ userId, email, recipeCount }]` via `prisma.user.findMany` + `_count.recipes`. Non-admin → **403**; unauth → 401.

### Step 6: Health + static serving + bootstrap
**Files:** `backend/src/health/health.controller.ts`, `backend/src/app.module.ts`, `backend/src/main.ts`
**Change:** `/api/health` returns `{status:'ok'}`; `/api/health/deep` runs `SELECT 1`. `ServeStaticModule.forRoot({ rootPath: <angular dist>, exclude: ['/api/(.*)'] })` with SPA fallback. `main.ts`: `setGlobalPrefix('api')`, global `ValidationPipe({whitelist,transform})`, CORS enabled, listen 80.

### Step 7: Angular core (auth, guards, interceptor, services)
**Files:** `frontend/src/app/core/*`, `app.config.ts`
**Change:** `AuthService` (login/register/logout, token in `localStorage`, `isAdmin`, `me()`). `authInterceptor` attaches Bearer. `authGuard` redirects to `/login`; `adminGuard` redirects non-admins to `/recipes`. `RecipeService` + `AdminService` hit `${apiBase}/recipes` and `${apiBase}/admin/overview`.

### Step 8: Angular pages & routing
**Files:** `frontend/src/app/pages/*`, `app.routes.ts`, `app.component.*`, `src/index.html`
**Change:** Title `FaithfulG Recipe Box`; header shows **FaithfulG** branding + logout. Routes (each with `data.flow`):
- `/login` (public, `flow: 'login'`), `/signup` (public, `flow: 'signup'`)
- `/recipes` (`authGuard`, `flow: 'recipes-list'`) — list items carry `data-testid="recipe-item"`
- `/recipes/new` (`authGuard`, `flow: 'recipe-create'`) — create form
- `/recipes/:id/edit` (`authGuard`, `flow: 'recipe-edit'`) — edit/delete
- `/admin` (`adminGuard`, `flow: 'admin-overview'`) — table with `data-testid="admin-overview"`
- `''` → redirect `/recipes`; `**` → redirect. Reactive forms for create/login/signup.

### Step 9: Dockerfile & entrypoint
**Files:** `Dockerfile`, `docker-entrypoint.sh`
**Change:** Stage 1 `node:20-alpine` build Angular (`ng build --base-href /faithful-e2e-g/`). Stage 2 build NestJS (`nest build`, `prisma generate`). Runtime: `node:20-alpine`, copy `dist/`, `node_modules`, `prisma/`, Angular `dist` into served path; `EXPOSE 80`; `ENTRYPOINT` runs entrypoint (migrate deploy → seed → start).

### Step 10: Kubernetes Postgres + wiring
**Files:** `k8s/postgres.yaml`, `k8s/deployment.yaml`, `kustomization.yaml`
**Change:** Postgres Deployment (small requests), ClusterIP `postgres:5432`, PVC. App Deployment gets `env` for `DATABASE_URL=postgresql://app:...@postgres:5432/app` and `JWT_SECRET` (via `app-secrets`), plus readiness/liveness probes on `/api/health`. Add postgres to kustomization resources.

## Testing Strategy
- **Backend unit/e2e:** register→login returns JWT; `GET /api/recipes` without token → **401**; create "Banitsa" then list contains it; non-admin `GET /api/admin/overview` → **403**; admin → 200 with counts; `/api/health/deep` → 200 when DB up.
- **Seed:** run seed, assert `SEED_CREDS_JSON` printed and both accounts log in.
- **Frontend:** log in as demo user, create "Banitsa", assert element with `data-testid="recipe-item"`; log in as admin, open `/admin`, assert `data-testid="admin-overview"`; verify page title/header contain "FaithfulG"; unauthenticated visit to `/recipes` redirects to `/login`.
- **Deploy smoke:** through ingress, `/faithful-e2e-g/api/health` → 200 and SPA loads with correct base-href.

## Risks
- **DB provisioning under namespace memory quota** — Postgres + app must fit; use minimal resource requests, `Recreate` strategy, and a modest PVC. If PVC/StorageClass is unavailable, fall back to `emptyDir` (data non-persistent) — flag for infra.
- **Base-href/ingress coupling** — wrong `--base-href` breaks asset loading; must stay `/faithful-e2e-g/` and API calls relative.
- **Migrations at boot** — `migrate deploy` must run before listen; a failed migration should crash the container (surfaces via readiness probe) rather than serve a broken app.
- **First-boot ordering** — app must wait for Postgres; rely on readiness probe + restart, or an init wait-for-db loop in the entrypoint.
